title: rosa canina var. glaberrima
intro: Rosa canina var. glaberrima, also known as the dwarf or smooth wild rose, is a variety of the Rosa canina species, a type of rose native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, attributed to its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Rosa canina var. glaberrima include supporting cardiovascular health, promoting digestive well-being, and potentially aiding in the treatment of various conditions such as diarrhea, gastritis, and menstrual disorders. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. glaberrima include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make supplements, tinctures, and topical creams.
